Rudolph "Rudi" Caracciola considered greatest Grand Prix drivers, winning more than races during career years, claiming three European Driving Championships, equivalent today's Formula World Drivers Championship.
native Remagen, Germany, Caracciola discovered love automobiles early age, became apprentice Farnir auto works Dresden.
After World became salesman Farnir, began racing their cars promote sales.
fell love with speed, tried gain attention factory-backed teams Mercedes-Benz elsewhere.
first race 1923 4-cylinder Berlin Stadium.
Caracciola began driving Mercedes some their less significant races, 1926 break.
With factory team Spain important race, there enter initial German Grand Prix.
talked Mercedes into loaning car, went driving rain.
joined factory team soon after After winning several races over next four years, Caracciola when Depression forced Mercedes suspend their racing operation.
bought with last savings, Irish Grand Prix.
1935, back with Mercedes, first European tide, beating young Auto Union driver named Bernd Rosemeyer.
next years, Caracciola Rosemeyer swapped European championships, with Rudi prevailing again 1937.
friendship ended tragically when were chasing world speed records 1938 Autobahn, Rosemeyer's crashed exploded.
Caracciola went third championship, something gone from driving.
1939, Caracciola again German fled Switzerland retired when Hitler tried "honor" with Nazi title.
race again until 1946, when entered Indy 500, severely injured crash while trying qualify.
After that raced limited basis, also high-speed testing Mercedes.
raced last time sports race Berne, when career ended another crash.
died cancer Kassell, Germany, 1959.
